question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted YAML/environment-variable payload into the `hiero.mirror.monitor.subscribe` namespace to bypass the `validate()` method's name-only checks and indirectly cause downstream `maxTransactionFee` values to be set outside design parameters, leading to operator funds being drained at an unexpected rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a subscribe scenario name that collides with a publish scenario name to confuse the `Sets.intersection(grpc.keySet(), rest.keySet())` check, causing the monitor to silently skip fee validation on a linked publish scenario and submit transactions with an unbounded `maxTransactionFee`?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `validate()` only checks for empty/duplicate scenario names and never validates fee-related fields in nested `GrpcSubscriberProperties` or `RestSubscriberProperties`, allowing fee parameters to be set to `Long.MAX_VALUE` without triggering any exception?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `hiero.mirror.monitor.subscribe.grpc.<name>` entry with a blank-space-only name that passes `StringUtils.isBlank` detection inconsistently across JVM locales, bypassing the `validate()` guard and injecting fee-modifying properties into the grpc scenario map?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `enabled=false` to bypass the `if (enabled && grpc.isEmpty() && rest.isEmpty())` guard in `validate()`, then inject fee-altering properties into the `grpc` or `rest` maps that are still processed by downstream components, causing transaction fees to be modified outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a null key in the `grpc` map (as tested in `SubscribePropertiesTest.nullName()`) to cause `StringUtils.isBlank(null)` to return true and throw an `IllegalArgumentException`, creating a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate fee-capped scenarios from loading while a fee-uncapped scenario remains active?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a very large number of entries into the `grpc` map (no size limit enforced in `SubscribeProperties`) to exhaust memory during `Sets.union(grpc.keySet(), rest.keySet())` computation in `validate()`, causing a DoS that prevents fee-capped scenarios from being validated and loaded?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any upper-bound check on the `grpc` or `rest` map sizes in `validate()` to inject thousands of subscribe scenarios, each referencing a publish scenario with an elevated `maxTransactionFee`, causing the operator account to be charged fees far beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `rest` map entry whose name exactly matches a `grpc` map entry to trigger the `Sets.intersection` duplicate-name exception in `validate()`, preventing the monitor from starting and forcing an operator to use a fallback configuration with no `maxTransactionFee` cap?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `grpc.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` call in `validate()` to overwrite a previously set scenario name with a crafted value that bypasses downstream fee-validation logic that relies on scenario name matching?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `GrpcSubscriberProperties` entry with a `topicId` pointing to a topic whose messages encode fee-override instructions, and since `validate()` does not check `topicId` content, cause the monitor to process fee-altering messages outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to `Duration.ofSeconds(1L)` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds = 1L)`) to cause the monitor to log subscription statistics so frequently that it masks fee-anomaly alerts, allowing fee modifications outside design parameters to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to an extremely large `Duration` value (no upper bound enforced) to suppress fee-anomaly logging entirely, allowing transaction fees modified outside design parameters to remain undetected for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@ConfigurationProperties(\

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit Spring Boot's relaxed binding for `@ConfigurationProperties` to supply `HIERO_MIRROR_MONITOR_SUBSCRIBE_GRPC_TEST_MAX_TRANSACTION_FEE` (with underscores) as an environment variable that maps to a fee field in a nested `GrpcSubscriberProperties`, bypassing the `@Min(1)` constraint and setting fees to 0 or negative values?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to the maximum allowed value of 1024 (via `@Max(1024)`) to create 1024 SDK clients, each submitting transactions with the default `maxTransactionFee` of `1_000_000_000` tinybars, causing the operator to pay fees at a rate 1024x beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to 1024 and simultaneously inject 1024 `grpc` scenarios, each with `subscribers` set to 1024 (the `@Max(1024)` limit in `AbstractSubscriberProperties`), creating over 1 million concurrent subscriptions that each trigger fee-bearing transactions outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on `statusFrequency` to set it to `Long.MAX_VALUE` nanoseconds, causing an integer overflow in `Duration` arithmetic that results in a negative or zero frequency, disabling fee-anomaly status reporting entirely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `RestSubscriberProperties` entry with `samplePercent=0.0` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(0)`) to disable transaction verification entirely, allowing fee-modified transactions to be submitted without any mirror-node validation, making fee manipulation outside design parameters undetectable?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `RestSubscriberProperties` entry with `timeout` set to the minimum 500ms (`@DurationMin(millis = 500)`) to cause all REST verification calls to time out, preventing fee-anomaly detection while fee-modified transactions continue to be submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `RestSubscriberProperties` entry with no `publishers` set (empty `LinkedHashSet`) to cause the subscriber to never match any published transaction, effectively disabling fee verification for all publish scenarios and allowing fee modifications outside design parameters to go unchecked?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RestSubscriberProperties.getLimit()` override that returns `Long.MAX_VALUE` when `limit=0` to create an unbounded subscription loop that continuously queries the REST API for fee-modified transactions, causing resource exhaustion on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `GrpcSubscriberProperties` constructor that sets `retry.setMaxAttempts(Long.MAX_VALUE)` to create a scenario that retries indefinitely when a fee-modified transaction fails, causing the operator to be charged retry fees far beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to a past timestamp (no lower-bound validation) to cause the gRPC subscriber to replay all historical topic messages, including any that encode fee-override instructions, and process them as if they were current fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to a future timestamp to cause `getEndTime()` (which returns `startTime.plus(duration)`) to overflow `Instant.MAX`, triggering an `ArithmeticException` that crashes the subscriber and prevents fee-capped scenarios from running?
